page: Amazon Web Services c7gn.12xlarge is an ARM64-based compute-optimized server powered by 48 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor. Operating at 2.6 GHz, this architecture allocates exactly one thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of memory, yielding a compute-optimized allocation of 2.0 GB per core. It features no local storage or GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU, Geekbench multi-core, and software compilation tests scoring in the top 25%. Memory subsystem performance is notable, with Passmark memory scores reaching the top 10% tier, and large-block read/write memory bandwidth benchmarks placing in the top 25%. Single-core performance, including stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core, falls within the middle 50% average.

The server delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps, supporting high-throughput network and EBS-optimized operations. This network capacity, combined with strong multi-core processing and top-tier memory bandwidth, makes the instance suitable for network-intensive workloads, static web serving, database operations, and software compilation. However, the lack of AVX/AMX instruction sets and local NVMe storage represents a tradeoff for workloads requiring local scratch space or specialized x86 vector instructions.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and includes no local storage or GPU accelerators. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong multi-core CPU capabilities and top-tier memory bandwidth, with Passmark memory scores in the top 10% and stress-ng multi-core performance in the top 25%. Conversely, single-core metrics and LLM inference speeds remain in the average middle 50% tier. This profile makes the c7gn.12xlarge highly effective for network-intensive applications, software compilation, and database operations, though the absence of local NVMe storage and x86 vector instructions are notable tradeoffs.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server with 48 Graviton cores, 96 GB RAM, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 48 Graviton cores, 96 GB RAM, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring dedicated physical cores, high-speed memory bandwidth, and 150 Gbps baseline network performance.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 ARM64 physical cores
Configured with 96.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
Delivers a high baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps
Strong multi-core CPU and top-tier memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Best suited for network-intensive workloads, software compilation, and database operations
Tradeoffs include lack of local NVMe storage and x86 vector instructions
